privacyPolicy.inAppLocation: Settings > AirVision M1 > App Preferences > Privacy Policy

appAccess.reviewAccessInstructions: OpenClaw HUD can be reviewed without a live gateway by opening Settings > AirVision M1 > App Preferences and enabling Demo Mode. Gateway pairing is optional for the HUD surface. If Google Play review needs live gateway features, provide a temporary setup code or demo gateway credentials in the Play Console App access form only; do not store those credentials in this repository.

targetAudience.rationale: OpenClaw HUD is a developer/operator assistant HUD for Samsung DeX and wearable display workflows, not a children-directed app.

contentRating.notes: No gambling, financial trading, dating, UGC social feed, or ads. Assistant responses depend on the user-configured OpenClaw gateway and selected model/provider.

dataDeletion.userDeletionPath: Android Settings > Apps > OpenClaw HUD > Storage > Clear data, or uninstall the app.
dataDeletion.notes: The HUD app does not create an OpenClaw cloud account. Gateway accounts, logs, and model-provider data are controlled by the user's configured gateway/provider.

dataSafety.dataEncryptedInTransitNotes: Public gateway URLs require wss:// or Tailscale Serve. The app also permits cleartext ws:// only for localhost, Android emulator, private LAN, link-local, and Tailnet-style addresses so developers can connect to self-hosted gateways. Do not answer that all collected user data is encrypted in transit unless these self-hosted cleartext paths are disabled before submission.

collectedData.whereUsed: Wake word, push-to-talk, and realtime caption/translation workflows when enabled by the user.
collectedData.destination: Configured OpenClaw gateway and the model/provider selected through that gateway.

collectedData.whereUsed: HUD chat messages, assistant run status, active session/provider selections, and notification summaries when enabled.
Local gateway connection state, device capability status, AirVision profile settings, and diagnostics exports created explicitly by the user.
collectedData.destination: Stored locally unless the user exports diagnostics or connects to a gateway.

reviewEvidence.demoMode: Settings > AirVision M1 > App Preferences > Demo Mode
airVisionCompanionReviewSteps: Settings > AirVision M1 > App Preferences > Demo Mode
Settings > AirVision M1 > Windows Spatial & Mirror Controls > Cast
Settings > AirVision M1 > Windows Spatial & Mirror Controls > Display
Settings > AirVision M1 > Firmware Updates > Export
Settings > AirVision M1 > Windows App Handoff > Export
Settings > AirVision M1 > Diagnostics Export > Export

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.capability: HUD presentation and DeX display targeting
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Enable Demo Mode, then open the HUD tab or launch the HUD presentation.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Fallback screenshots and CI exercise the phone review path; live M1 testing is only needed before claiming display-specific framing on hardware.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 profile controls, profile backup/import, and Windows App Handoff export.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Saved Android values, per-profile runtime summaries, and export text are generated without raw USB serials.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 profile brightness, HUD dimming preview, and M1 touch-bar hardware brightness.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Software HUD dimming is reviewable offline; live M1 testing proves panel brightness behavior and firmware touch-bar passthrough.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 profile distance and HUD scale controls.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Virtual HUD distance scaling, per-profile HUD scale, and runtime summaries are generated without a live M1.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.capability: IPD, fit, clarity, and text size
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 IPD, Fit & Clarity guidance, and diagnostics export.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: IPD calibration defaults to 67 mm and fit guidance is reviewable offline; physical focus proof needs the live M1.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.capability: Splendid, Eye Care, and blue-light filter
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 Splendid and Eye Care profile controls.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Android previews HUD color/warmth states offline; true panel preset writes need ASUS HID protocol evidence.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.capability: Motion Sync, 3D Mode, and Light Load Mode
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 Motion Sync, 3D Mode, Light Load Mode, and runtime diagnostics.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Light Load trims Android HUD work offline; Motion Sync and panel 3D writes remain firmware-protocol gated.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 > Gesture & Hotkey Settings.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Single-tap, double-tap, swipe, brightness-key, and media-key mappings are stored offline; live M1 proof is needed for firmware-delivered events.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 > Firmware Link and Diagnostics Export.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Offline demo shows blocked/write-readiness states; connected M1 adds live descriptor and HID report-path context.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 > Firmware Updates > Export.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Android write actions remain blocked until sanitized ASUS HID capture evidence and live M1 write tests are both present.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.capability: Samsung/native captions and OpenClaw translation fallback
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Voice tab caption controls and Samsung/native caption launcher.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Native captioning is delegated to Android/Samsung UI; OpenClaw fallback defaults to sage-router fast profile with thinking off.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 > App Preferences and Profile Backup.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Startup view, display target, language, speaker, native captions, translation languages, demo mode, privacy/support links, and JSON backup/import are reviewable offline.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 > Windows App Handoff > Export.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: The Android app exports Cyber/Windows ASUS AirVision apply steps, runtime summaries, gesture mappings, and privacy reminders without raw USB serials.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 > Firmware Link and diagnostics export.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: USB manufacturer/product, vendor/product ID, descriptor version, and interface details populate when the M1 is connected.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 > Identify or Demo Mode HUD presentation.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Android can show a temporary HUD 1 marker on the Presentation display for reviewer and external-display identification.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 > Display Target, presentation routing diagnostics, and DeX launch script.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: External-display targeting and physical main-screen visibility are configurable offline; final DeX/M1 topology proof needs a live external display.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 > Windows-only spatial controls and Windows App Handoff export.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Cursor Follow, Center Cursor, and 3DoF are reported as Windows-only; Android provides distance hotkey fallback state only.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.capability: Unity mirror window / projected glasses view
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 > Cast, Display, and Windows App Handoff mirror fallback guidance.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: The ASUS Unity mirror window and Ctrl+Alt+E shortcut stay Windows-only; Android provides Cast, Display, and DeX sharing fallback guidance.

reviewEvidence.airVisionCompanionReviewNotes: Demo Mode lets reviewers verify the 18-feature AirVision companion HUD catalog without a live gateway or live M1. Cast and Display open Android or DeX mirror fallback settings outside the HUD. Firmware-update handoff, Windows app handoff, and diagnostics exports are user-initiated files that omit raw USB serial values; Android firmware writes remain blocked until validated ASUS HID protocol evidence exists.

finalSubmission.notes: Fill these external Play Console artifacts before running scripts/verify-play-submission-package.mjs --final and publish-play-internal.mjs --commit. Local screenshot paths listed here are validated as Play-compliant JPEG or 24-bit PNG files.
